edge_hypothesis: Ichimoku TK cross is an ACCEPT (PF=1.604) using fast Tenkan/Kijun averages. The Ichimoku cloud twist (ISA crossing ISB) is a fundamentally different, SLOWER structural signal — it occurs when the forward-projected equilibrium levels realign, indicating a deeper macro regime shift. Cloud twists are rarer than TK crosses but represent higher-conviction regime changes. KAMA adapts its speed to noise levels — when price is above KAMA during a bullish cloud twist, adaptive momentum confirms the structural shift. This combines two proven adaptive concepts (Ichimoku structure + KAMA speed-adaptation) at different timescales. The cloud has never been tested as a standalone entry signal. If slower Ichimoku components produce cleaner entries than TK, this opens a new family of Ichimoku-structural strategies.

variants.name: iku_cloud_kama_twist_8to1
variants.description: Core variant. Cloud twist (ISA crosses ISB) as macro regime signal + KAMA adaptive confirmation. 8:1 R:R sweet spot, 1.5 ATR stop.
exit_rules: Exit on opposite cloud twist signal.
Exit on max bars in trade threshold.
risk_rules: Risk note: ATR-based stop and TP for deterministic execution.
Risk note: risk per trade target is 1%.
8:1 R:R proven sweet spot across all ACCEPTs.

components.notes: Cloud twist (ISA/ISB cross) as macro structural regime shift — slower and higher-conviction than TK cross
Adaptive moving average confirms price momentum aligns with structural regime change

exit_rules: Exit on opposite cloud twist signal.

risk_rules: Risk note: ATR-based stop and TP for deterministic execution.
Risk note: risk per trade target is 1%.
10:1 R:R — wider target for high-conviction structural moves.

components.notes: Cloud twist as macro structural regime shift

variants.name: iku_cloud_twist_solo
variants.description: Single-condition cloud twist. Tests whether the cloud twist alone (without KAMA confirmation) has sufficient edge. If this works, cloud twist is a standalone regime-change signal. Minimal complexity.
exit_rules: Exit on max bars in trade threshold.
risk_rules: Risk note: ATR-based stop and TP for deterministic execution.
Risk note: risk per trade target is 1%.
8:1 R:R — single condition entry for maximum simplicity.

components.notes: Cloud twist as standalone regime-change signal — tests whether structural Ichimoku signals work without confirmation

advisory_context.novel_element: FIRST cloud-component Ichimoku test — ISA/ISB twist is a different signal family from TK cross (ACCEPT PF=1.604). Tests slower structural regime detection.
